findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "get_critical_path" is 611 chars, far longer than this server's median (83). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

description: Calculate the critical path of a project on the Ganty side and return it as data (don't ask the model to reason over raw task lists). Uses forward/backward CPM with progress-aware remaining duration. Returns critical_path tasks in order, per-task earliest/latest start/finish + slack, project_end_dat

labels.name: reschedule_and_propagate
labels.description: Compute the downstream impact of shifting a single task. Default is dry_run (no DB writes); set mode='commit' to actually persist the changes. Cascades forward only (no pull): successors are pushed only if their predecessor's new end + lag would violate their current start. Tasks with progress=100 a

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_milestones · Private data: reschedule_and_propagate · Public sink: get_critical_path, reschedule_and_propagate · Destructive: delete_project, create_task, delete_task.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_milestones · Private data: reschedule_and_propagate · Public sink: get_critical_path, reschedule_and_propagate · Destructive: delete_project, create_task, delete_task

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
